intro: There's no new math here — the digits NEVER change. Only their seats do. ×10 slides everything one place left; ÷10 slides right. That's all place value is.

challenges.say: ×10 promotes every digit one seat left. Same digits, richer seats.

challenges.prompt: Now send it back: make 47 become 4.7
challenges.say: ÷10 slides them right. ×10 and ÷10 are undo-partners.
Two ×10 hops = ×100. Zeros appear when the digits run out of seats.

challenges.prompt: Find 10% of $84 — that's exactly one ÷10 hop
challenges.say: 10% means ÷10. $8.40 — THE tip-and-tax trick, one button.
$9 of tax. Double it for a 20% tip: $18. Restaurant math, solved standing up.
1% = ÷100 = two hops. And % literally MEANS ÷100 — same thing!

challenges.prompt: Turn 62 into 0.62 — this is how 62% becomes a decimal
challenges.say: 62% = 62 ÷ 100 = 0.62. Percent-to-decimal is just a two-hop ride.
Three ×10 hops = ×1000. Watch zeros get born as digits run out.
Three hops up. Every hop is ×10 — that's the whole secret of our number system.
